Does mortality rate decrease as the years go by?

What countries would require the most medical funding and research? (this question would require insight on deaths NOT caused by natural causes or “Accident”)

Graphs

Does mortality rate decrease as the years go by?

What countries would require the most medical funding and research?

The Total Disease Death Count from 1990 - 2015

Ever wondered how much death occurs just by disease alone? As we know the population is rapidly growing, but is there a growth in disease death as well?

All Diseases

What are the Diseases we are referring to?

##  [1] "Meningitis"                                
##  [2] "Alzheimer s Disease and Other Dementias"   
##  [3] "Parkinson s Disease"                       
##  [4] "Nutritional Deficiencies"

##  [8] "Cardiovascular Diseases"

## [11] "Diarrheal Diseases"

## [17] "Cirrhosis and Other Chronic Liver Diseases"
## [18] "Digestive Diseases"                        
## [19] "Acute Hepatitis"

Comparing Graphs

How has the death amount changed over the years for each specific diseases?

Lets simplify further more to just the top 5 disease with the highest death counts.

In summary, the number one deaths by a disease is Cardiovascular Disease.

## Warning: There was 1 warning in `summarise()`.
## ℹ In argument: `across(Meningitis:Acute.Hepatitis, sum, na.rm = TRUE)`.
## ℹ In group 1: `Continent = "Africa"`.

If I Were A Reasercher, Where Would I Go?

This graph shows the years: 1990, 2000, 2010, 2015, and comparing the leading cause of death by Continent.

As you can see in all four years, Asia stood on top. Also, cardiovascular was the highest disease for every continent in 2010 and 2015.

Deeper Dive Into Asia

Here is a visual map of Asia’s Cardiovascular Deaths, labeling the top 5 Countries.
